description: Draft of a court record dated 623 (1226) written on a very small piece of paper (4 x 2.5 in) regarding the testimony of Abū Saʿīd al-Levi b. Abū l-Maʿānī that Abū l-Faḍl b. Mukhtār al-Iskandarī had died while in Kalah (on the west coast of present-day Malaysia, although the precise location is debated). The document establishes that India traders ventured to Southeast Asia. Goitein didn't supply a reading for the place-name after Kalah ("in the lands of ..."); Marina Rustow reads "Java" (al-Jāwa), a broad Arabic term for Southeast Asia, a reading confirmed by Michael Laffan. The document gives dates according to the Jewish (Seleucid) calendar and the hijrī calendar. Old IB Number: 322. New IB Number: VII, 32. In the hand of Avraham Maimonides. Other date mentioned in document: 10 Tammuz 1537 (Seleucid)
tags: Eastern Indian Ocean, India, Avraham Maimonides autograph, Avraham Maimonides, IB7, Kadaram, Kedah, Kalah, Malaysia
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-13. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
